info.title: Rewrd API

tags.name: Customers
tags.description: Manage your loyalty program customers. Create, update, list, and control the status of customers enrolled in your points program.

tags.name: Points
tags.description: Credit and redeem loyalty points for customers. View transaction history and manage the points lifecycle.

tags.name: Merchant
tags.description: View and update your merchant configuration, earning rules, IP security settings, and analytics dashboard.

ErrorResponse.description: Standard error response returned by all endpoints on failure.

code.description: Machine-readable error code for programmatic handling.

request_id.description: Unique identifier for this request, useful for support inquiries.

Pagination.description: Pagination metadata included in list responses.

total.description: Total number of items across all pages.

Customer.description: Represents a customer enrolled in your loyalty program.

uid.description: Unique identifier for the customer, generated by Rewrd.

email.description: Customer's email address. Used for notifications if configured.

phone_number.description: Customer's phone number. This is the primary identifier used for matching.

date_of_birth.description: Customer's date of birth in ISO 8601 format.

status.description: Current status of the customer. `active`: can earn and redeem points. `restricted`: blocked from all point operations.

points_balance.description: Current available points balance for this customer.

created_at.description: Timestamp when the customer was first created.

MerchantConfig.description: Your merchant account configuration including profile, social links, point expiry settings, and security options.

email.description: Primary email address associated with the merchant account.

created_at.description: Timestamp when the merchant account was created.

point_balance.description: Your current merchant point pool balance. This is debited when customers earn points and credited when customers redeem points.

last_chance_email_countdown.description: Number of days before point expiry to send a last-chance notification email to the customer.

minimum_threshold_amount.description: Minimum point balance a customer must have to be eligible for redemption.

point_should_expire.description: Whether customer points expire after a set period.

reactivation_email_countdown.description: Number of days of customer inactivity before sending a re-engagement email.

point_expiration_date.description: Number of days after which customer points expire (only if `point_should_expire` is true).

minimum_threshold_updated_at.description: Timestamp of the last update to the minimum threshold amount.

status.description: Current status of the merchant account.

webhook_url.description: URL where Rewrd sends webhook notifications for events like `points.earned` and `points.redeemed`.

webhook_secret.description: Secret used to sign webhook payloads so you can verify their authenticity.

EarningRule.description: An earning rule defines how customers earn points. Rules are configured in the Rewrd dashboard and referenced by ID when crediting points.

id.description: Unique identifier for the earning rule.

merchant_id.description: The merchant this rule belongs to.

name.description: Human-readable name for the earning rule (e.g. "Purchase Reward", "Signup Bonus").

points.description: Number of points awarded when this rule is applied.

subtype.description: Sub-category for more granular rule classification.

status.description: Whether this rule is currently `active` or `inactive`.

users_rewarded.description: Total number of times this rule has been used to award points.

deleted.description: Whether this rule has been soft-deleted.

created_at.description: Timestamp when the rule was created.

updated_at.description: Timestamp of the last update to this rule.

earning_type.description: Whether points are awarded as a `fixed` amount or `percentage_off` of a purchase.

AnalyticsSummary.description: Aggregated analytics for your loyalty program over a specified time period.

period.description: The time period this data covers.

total_earned.description: Total points earned by all customers in this period.

total_redeemed.description: Total points redeemed by all customers in this period.

active_customers.description: Number of customers who had at least one transaction in this period.

total_points_balance.description: Combined points balance across all customers.

total_transactions.description: Total number of point transactions (credits + redemptions) in this period.

PointsTransaction.description: A ledger entry representing a points credit or redemption. Every point operation creates a transaction record.

customer_uid.description: The customer who earned or redeemed points.

points.description: Number of points in this transaction.

narration.description: Optional description or note for this transaction.

transaction_type.description:
Type of transaction:
- `member_points_adjustment_credit` — direct point credit
- `member_purchase_order_earned_fixed` — points earned via a fixed earning rule
- `member_purchase_order_earned_percentage` — points earned via a percentage earning rule
- `member_purchase_order_redeemed` — points redeemed

ledger_type.description: Generic type of ledger entry (credit or debit).

reference_id.description: Unique reference ID for this transaction, used for deduplication.

balance_before.description: Customer's points balance before this transaction was applied.

balance_after.description: Customer's points balance after this transaction was applied.

created_at.description: Timestamp when this transaction was created.

post.description:
Creates a new customer or updates an existing one. This is an **upsert** operation — meaning "update if exists, insert (create) if not".

**How the matching works:**
Rewrd uses the `phone_number` to check if a customer already exists in your merchant account:
- **New customer** (phone number not found) → A new customer record is created with status `active` and 0 points balance.
- **Existing customer** (phone number already exists) → Only the fields you provide are updated. Fields you don't include are left unchanged.

This makes it safe to call this endpoint every time a user signs up or logs in — you won't create duplicates.

---

**Quick Start — Enroll your first customer:**

```bash
curl -X POST https://api.getrewrd.com/v1/customers \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer sk_test_your_api_key" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -H "Idempotency-Key: enroll-jane-001" \
  -d '{
    "customer_email": "jane@example.com",
    "phone_number": "+2348012345678"
  }'
```

---

This endpoint requires an `Idempotency-Key` header to prevent duplicate customer creation from retried requests.

parameters.name: Idempotency-Key
parameters.description: Unique key for idempotent request handling. Must be alphanumeric with hyphens or underscores, 1–100 characters.

phone_number.description: Customer's phone number. Used as the unique identifier for matching.

400.description: Validation error — missing required fields or invalid `Idempotency-Key`.

401.description: Unauthorized — missing or invalid API key.

get.description:
Retrieves a paginated list of all customers belonging to your merchant account.

You can filter results by `email` or `phone_number` to quickly find specific customers. Results are sorted by creation date (newest first).

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Number of customers to return per page (max 100).

parameters.name: email
parameters.description: Filter customers by exact email address match.

parameters.name: phone_number
parameters.description: Filter customers by exact phone number match.

401.description: Unauthorized — missing or invalid API key.

get.description:
Retrieves the full details of a single customer by their unique identifier (`uid`).

Use this endpoint to check a customer's current points balance, status, and profile information before performing point operations.

parameters.name: uid
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the customer.

401.description: Unauthorized — missing or invalid API key.

404.description: Customer not found — no customer exists with the given UID.

put.description:
Updates the profile information of an existing customer. Only the fields provided in the request body are updated; omitted fields are left unchanged.

**Note:** The customer must have an `active` status to be updated. Restricted customers must be unrestricted first.

parameters.name: uid
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the customer to update.

date_of_birth.description: Updated date of birth in ISO 8601 format.

401.description: Unauthorized — missing or invalid API key.

403.description: Customer is not active — restricted customers cannot be updated.

patch.description:
Restricts a customer, preventing them from earning or redeeming points. The customer's existing points balance is preserved but frozen.

**When to use this:**
- **Fraud prevention** — You suspect a customer is abusing the program
- **Dispute resolution** — A customer disputes a transaction and you want to freeze their account while investigating
- **Temporary suspension** — The customer requested a pause on their account

**Customer status lifecycle:**
```
active ──(restrict)──► restricted ──(unrestrict)──► active
```

Only `active` customers can be restricted. If the customer is already `restricted`, this endpoint will return a `403` error.

parameters.name: uid
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the customer to restrict.

401.description: Unauthorized — missing or invalid API key.

403.description: Customer is not active — cannot restrict an already restricted or inactive customer.

patch.description: Removes the restriction on a customer, restoring their ability to earn and redeem points. Their previously frozen points balance becomes available again.

parameters.name: uid
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the customer to unrestrict.

401.description: Unauthorized — missing or invalid API key.

get.description:
Retrieves your current merchant configuration including profile information, social links, point expiry settings, webhook configuration, and security settings.

Use this endpoint to read your current settings before making updates.

401.description: Unauthorized — missing or invalid API key.

patch.description:
Updates your merchant configuration. Only the fields included in the request body are updated; omitted fields remain unchanged.

Common use cases include updating the minimum redemption threshold, enabling point expiration, or updating social media links.

minimum_threshold_amount.description: Minimum point balance required for redemption.

point_expiration_date.description: Number of days until points expire (only relevant if `point_should_expire` is true).

last_chance_email_countdown.description: Days before expiry to send a last-chance email.

reactivation_email_countdown.description: Days of inactivity before sending a re-engagement email.

401.description: Unauthorized — missing or invalid API key.

get.description:
Retrieves all active (non-deleted) earning rules for your merchant account.

**What are earning rules?**
Earning rules define the logic for how customers earn points. You create them in the Rewrd Dashboard, and then reference them by `id` when processing transactions via the [`POST /points/transaction`](#/Points/processPointsTransaction) endpoint.

**Understanding `earning_type`:**

| Earning Type | How points are calculated | Example |
|---|---|---|
| `fixed` | Awards a flat number of points regardless of order value | Rule says 50 points → Customer always gets 50 points, whether they spend ₦1,000 or ₦100,000 |
| `percentage_off` | Awards points as a percentage of the order value | Rule says 10% → Customer spends ₦5,000, earns 500 points |

Each rule also tracks `users_rewarded`, which is the total number of times it has been used to award points — useful for seeing which rules are most popular.

401.description: Unauthorized — missing or invalid API key.

get.description: Retrieves the details of a single earning rule by its ID, including usage statistics (`users_rewarded` count).

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The unique ID of the earning rule.

401.description: Unauthorized — missing or invalid API key.

404.description: Earning rule not found — no active rule exists with the given ID.

get.description:
Retrieves the list of IP addresses that are currently whitelisted for API access.

**How IP whitelisting works:**

IP whitelisting adds an extra layer of security by restricting which servers can make API calls with your key.

| Whitelist State | Behavior |
|---|---|
| **Empty list** (no IPs added) | All IP addresses are allowed — no restrictions |
| **Non-empty list** (one or more IPs) | **Only** requests from whitelisted IPs are accepted. All other IPs receive a `403 Forbidden` error. |

> ⚠️ **Be careful:** If you add IPs to the whitelist, make sure your server's IP is included, otherwise your own requests will be blocked!

**Tip:** You can find your server's public IP by running `curl ifconfig.me` in your terminal.

401.description: Unauthorized — missing or invalid API key.

post.description: Add a new IP address to the merchant's API whitelist.

name.description: A human-readable name for this IP

400.description: Validation error — invalid IP details provided.

401.description: Unauthorized — missing or invalid API key.

patch.description: Update details (name, status, etc) of a specific whitelisted IP.

delete.description: Remove an IP from the whitelist.

get.description:
Retrieves aggregated analytics for your loyalty program, filtered by time period.

**What each metric means:**

| Metric | What it tells you |
|---|---|
| `total_earned` | Total points given out to customers — higher means more customer engagement |
| `total_redeemed` | Total points spent by customers — higher means customers find your rewards valuable |
| `active_customers` | Customers who earned or redeemed at least once in the period — your "active base" |
| `total_points_balance` | Combined unspent points across all customers — this is your outstanding liability |
| `total_transactions` | Total number of credit + debit operations — measures overall program activity |

Use the `period` parameter to compare performance across different time windows (today, 7 days, 30 days, or all time).

parameters.name: period
parameters.description:
Time period to aggregate analytics for:
- `today` — current day only
- `7d` — last 7 days
- `30d` — last 30 days
- `all` — all time (default)

401.description: Unauthorized — missing or invalid API key.

post.description:
Process a points transaction for a customer. This single endpoint handles three scenarios:

**How it works:**
Use the `reward` and `redeem` boolean flags to tell Rewrd what type of transaction to perform. You can set one or both to `true`.

| `reward` | `redeem` | What happens |
|---|---|---|
| `true` | `false` | **Reward only** — Customer earns points based on an earning rule. |
| `false` | `true` | **Redeem only** — Customer spends points toward a purchase. |
| `true` | `true` | **Combined** — Customer earns AND redeems points in a single checkout. |

---

**When `reward` is `true`:**
You must provide `way_to_earn_id` (the ID of the earning rule from your dashboard). Rewrd will calculate the points to credit based on the rule's type:
- **Fixed rule** — Awards a fixed number of points regardless of `order_value` (e.g. always award 50 points).
- **Percentage rule** — Awards points as a percentage of `order_value` (e.g. 10% of ₦5,000 = 500 points).

**When `redeem` is `true`:**
You must provide `deduct_points` — the exact number of points to deduct from the customer's balance. The customer must have enough points, otherwise the request will fail with an `insufficient_points` error.

---

**Real-world example — Combined checkout:**
A customer buys a coffee for ₦5,000. They want to use 200 of their points AND earn new points for the purchase:
```json
{
  "customer_uid": "cust_abc123",
  "order_id": "ORD-003",
  "order_value": 5000,
  "redeem": true,
  "deduct_points": 200,
  "reward": true,
  "way_to_earn_id": 1
}
```
This will deduct 200 points AND credit new points based on earning rule #1, all in a single atomic operation.

---

This endpoint requires an `Idempotency-Key` header to prevent duplicate transactions. Use a unique key per checkout (e.g. your order ID).

parameters.name: Idempotency-Key
parameters.description: Unique key for idempotent request handling. Must be alphanumeric with hyphens or underscores, 1–100 characters.

customer_uid.description: The UID of the customer making the checkout.

order_id.description: External order ID tying this points usage to a checkout basket.

order_value.description: The fiat value of the items in the total basket.

redeem.description: True if the customer is choosing to deduct points towards the purchase.

reward.description: True if the order creates an earning opportunity.

deduct_points.description: Quantity of points exactly to be removed (required if redeem is true).

way_to_earn_id.description: Pre-configured loyalty mechanism integer rule (required if reward is true).

400.description: Validation error — missing required condition triggers.

401.description: Unauthorized — missing or invalid API key.

404.description: Customer or earning rule not found.

get.description:
Retrieves a paginated list of all point transactions (credits and redemptions) for a specific customer.

Transactions are sorted by date with the most recent first. Use this endpoint to display a customer's points activity history.

**Understanding the response fields:**

**`ledger_type`** tells you the direction of the transaction:
- `credit` — Points were **added** to the customer's balance
- `debit` — Points were **deducted** from the customer's balance

**`transaction_type`** tells you *how* the points were earned or spent:
| Transaction Type | Meaning |
|---|---|
| `member_points_adjustment_credit` | Points were directly credited (manual adjustment) |
| `member_purchase_order_earned_fixed` | Points earned via a **fixed** earning rule (e.g. "earn 50 points per purchase") |
| `member_purchase_order_earned_percentage` | Points earned via a **percentage** earning rule (e.g. "earn 10% of order value as points") |
| `member_purchase_order_redeemed` | Points were redeemed (spent) toward a purchase |

**`balance_before`** and **`balance_after`** show the customer's points balance before and after the transaction, so you can verify the math.

parameters.name: uid
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the customer.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Number of transactions to return per page.

401.description: Unauthorized — missing or invalid API key.
